metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact eval/sidecar command to run, the named test suite or fixture file(s), and the numeric pass-rate threshold (e.g. 'run `pytest tests/evals/reviewer_regression.py`; all N cases must pass at the same rate as baseline') so the regression check is concretely verifiable in-session.
findings.finding: The AC 'Eval/sidecar regression check: reworded prompts still catch what they caught' names no concrete proving command, no eval suite, no pass-rate threshold, and no definition of what 'catch what they caught' means quantitatively. An executor cannot verify this criterion in-session without invention.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval/sidecar regression check: reworded prompts still catch what they caught'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'affirmative-framing sweep over reviewer prompts' with a measurable criterion: reference the authoritative list (or file glob) of prompts in scope, state the compliance rule (e.g. each prohibition paired with an affirmative alternative, no unmitigated DO-NOT), and specify a verifiable check (e.g. a linter rule or manual audit table committed to the repo).
findings.finding: 'Affirmative-framing sweep over reviewer prompts' is effort-framed and lacks a measurable completion criterion — it states no observable outcome (e.g. 'all X prompts pass R-6 checklist'), no count of prompts in scope, and no definition of what a compliant vs non-compliant prompt looks like post-sweep.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Affirmative-framing sweep over reviewer prompts'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact file/section where the review-time checklist will be documented (e.g. `docs/reviewer-prompt-authoring.md`, a section in the existing contributor guide, or inline in the shared scaffolding header) so the AC has a concrete proving check.
findings.finding: 'Review-time checklist habit documented' does not specify where the checklist will live (which file, section, or artifact), leaving a real design choice deferred to the executor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Review-time checklist habit documented'; also What section R-6

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a What-section bullet describing the eval/sidecar regression check mechanism (e.g. which harness to run, what baseline to compare against, pass/fail threshold) so AC3 maps to described work.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Eval/sidecar regression check: reworded prompts still catch what they caught') has no corresponding described work in the What section explaining HOW the regression check will be executed — the What section describes only the prompt changes (R-6, G-12, FP-3(c)) with no bullet for the eval/sidecar mechanism itself.
findings.location: What section (no eval bullet) vs Acceptance Criteria AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet under What such as: 'Regression: run existing eval harness (or named sidecar) against reworded prompts; confirm per-criterion catch-rates match baseline within N% tolerance before merging.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for prompt rewrites (e.g. keep prior prompt text in version control with a named tag/branch, or apply changes incrementally per-prompt with per-prompt regression gate) so that a failing AC3 check has a defined remediation path.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges behavioral-drift risk from rewriting ~41 prompts and adds a regression check (AC3), but provides no rollback or back-out path if that check reveals regressions — T4 requires an explicit reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement.
findings.location: Why section ('Reword risks behavioral drift -> eval-guarded') and AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add to What or AC: 'Rollback: prompt changes are applied and committed incrementally (one rule group at a time); a regression on any group reverts only that group's changes via git revert of the relevant commit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback/remediation step to the regression AC: specify what action is taken (revert, flag for manual fix, partial-revert) if any reworded prompt fails the eval regression check, and confirm the sweep is idempotent under re-execution.
findings.finding: The plan mutates shared scaffolding and ~41 reviewer prompts (shared state consumed by the eval system) but specifies no rollback or recovery path if the regression check fails — an agent implementing this has no defined action when reworded prompts regress eval scores.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval/sidecar regression check: reworded prompts still catch what they caught'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: 'If regression check fails for any prompt, that prompt's rewrite is reverted to the prior version and flagged for manual review; the sweep may be re-run safely (idempotent: already-hardened prompts are skipped).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Sharpen the two vague ACs to be end-state-observable: specify a coverage criterion for the framing sweep (e.g., 'all prompts in the reviewer prompt library reviewed; DO-NOT constraints reduced by X% or justified in a comment') and a measurable pass bar for the regression check (e.g., 'all existing eval test cases pass with no new failures introduced').
findings.finding: Two AC items lack observable, end-state-verifiable pass criteria: 'Affirmative-framing sweep over reviewer prompts' does not define completion (e.g., all N prompts reviewed, zero remaining DO-NOT constraints, or a sampling threshold), and 'Eval/sidecar regression check' does not state what a passing result looks like (all tests green? no net loss of findings caught? a specific threshold).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Affirmative-framing sweep over reviewer prompts' and 'Eval/sidecar regression check'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete improvement criterion alongside the parity check — e.g., a count of DO-NOT / negative-instruction occurrences before vs. after the sweep, or a documented spot-evaluation showing reduced false-positive priming on a sample of real review inputs.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism demonstrates that the prompt-hardening produces an observable improvement; the sole AC ('reworded prompts still catch what they caught') validates only parity, not that the DO-NOT accumulation problem is actually reduced or that false-positive/negative rates improve after the sweep.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval/sidecar regression check: reworded prompts still catch what they caught'
findings.suggested_fix: Before the sweep, record a baseline count of DO-NOT/negative-instruction clauses across all reviewer prompts. Add an AC: 'Post-sweep count of prohibitive clauses is ≤ X% of baseline' (or similar). This turns the hygiene goal into a measurable outcome rather than a parity-only check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'SHARED scaffolding' means editing each relevant prompt .md file individually (finder, both verifiers, container, ISF) or building a new preamble-injection layer — and confirm no new prompt-composition infrastructure is needed before implementing G-12.
findings.finding: The plan's 'SHARED scaffolding (system layer, above ingested material)' framing implies a single injection point above multiple prompt bodies, but the codebase has no such shared-preamble composition layer: `resolve_prompt_cached` / `load_prompt` in `src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py` flatten each prompt to a single .md file's body with no multi-file preamble injection. An executor who reads 'shared scaffolding' as 'build a new composition layer' would be adding infrastructure the codebase does not have (NIH/over-engineering), while an executor who reads it as 'edit finder/verifier/container/ISF .md files individually' would be doing simple prose editing. The plan should clarify which is intended.
findings.location: What section — G-12 item: 'one prompt-injection directive in SHARED scaffolding (system layer, above ingested material)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'one prompt-injection directive in SHARED scaffolding (system layer, above ingested material)' with 'add the injection directive verbatim to each of the relevant prompt .md files (plan_review_finder.md, plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md, plan_review_container.md, plan_review_isf_finder.md) — no new composition infrastructure required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the '~41 reviewer prompts' count: grep `schema_version: 1` in `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/` returns ~59 prompt files; confirm whether code-review overlays and standalone reviewers (e.g. `code_quality.md`, `completion_verifier.md`, `ticket_quality.md`) are in-scope and update the sweep scope accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '~41 reviewer prompts' but the actual count is ~59 files with schema_version:1 in src/rebar/llm/reviewers/. The ~44% undercount is unverified by the plan and could lead an implementer to scope the sweep incorrectly.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '~41' with the actual count (currently 59) or explicitly list out-of-scope prompt categories (e.g. 'plan-review criteria + pass prompts only, not code-review overlays') so the sweep boundary is unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and correct the '(already in E1/F1)' parenthetical: E1 does not contain the forward-looking evaluation rule — confirm whether E1 should also receive it (or note it is intentionally absent from E1) before using this as a DRY rationale.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the forward-looking rule is 'already in E1/F1', but E1 (plan_review_E1.md) contains no such language — only F1 carries 'evaluate the spec AS WRITTEN, not the current codebase'. Treating E1 as a prior bearer of this rule is incorrect and weakens the DRY rationale for the proposed change.
findings.location: What section, FP-3(c) bullet: '(DRY; already in E1/F1)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change '(DRY; already in E1/F1)' to '(DRY; already in F1)' or, if E1 should also carry the rule, add it to the What section as a discrete edit target.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define R-6, G-12, and FP-3(c) concretely in the plan body (or cite the file/section where each is defined), since none of these identifiers exist anywhere in the codebase today and an executor cannot act on them as written.
findings.finding: The plan references 'R-6 (organizing principle)', 'G-12', and 'FP-3(c)' as if they are established, look-up-able rule identifiers, but none of these labels exist anywhere in the codebase, docs, or criteria registry — they are novel identifiers introduced only by this plan with no prior definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'SHARED scaffolding' is a new file requiring prompt-assembly loader changes (name the files in prompts.py/operations.py/workflow/runs.py that would need updating) or inline preamble additions to each named existing prompt (list the ~5–6 target files explicitly).
findings.finding: The plan's 'G-12: one prompt-injection directive in SHARED scaffolding (system layer)' names 'SHARED scaffolding' as a target artifact, but no such file exists in src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ or anywhere else in the codebase. If this is a new file the plan proposes creating, it is a CREATION (new behavior) — out-of-scope for a hardening-pass plan — and would require the prompt assembly layer (prompts.py, operations.py, workflow/runs.py) to explicitly load it, which the plan does not enumerate. If it instead means embedding a preamble section in each of the named existing prompts (finder/verifier/container/ISF/agentic criteria), those files are in-scope but the plan's 'system layer' framing implies a structural loader change.
findings.location: What section, G-12 bullet: 'one prompt-injection directive in SHARED scaffolding (system layer, above ingested material)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each of R-6, G-12, and FP-3(c): either add a file path / section heading where the rule is defined, or confirm these are plan-local labels (in which case rename them to avoid implying a lookup-able registry).
findings.finding: The rule identifiers R-6, G-12, and FP-3(c) are used as if they are established, lookup-able rule codes ('R-6 (organizing principle)', 'G-12', 'FP-3(c)'), but zero matches for any of these identifiers exist anywhere in the codebase, docs, or criteria registry. An implementer cannot look them up to understand their full specification.
findings.location: What section: 'R-6 (organizing principle)', 'G-12', 'FP-3(c)'; AC1: 'Shared-scaffolding injection directive + forward-looking rule'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the 'Why' or 'What' section explaining why the injection directive and forward-looking rule belong in the shared scaffolding prompts (finder/verifier/container/ISF) rather than in each criterion prompt — e.g. that criterion prompts are ingested as rubric text in the `instructions` field (not as system prompts), so only the shared scaffold reliably sits above all ingested material in the system layer.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section articulates the problem (DO-NOT accumulation, T8 critique) but states no positive rationale for WHY the chosen structural approach — a single centralized shared-scaffolding injection directive rather than per-prompt placement — is the right architectural fit for this codebase.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Why' section: 'Centralized placement in the shared scaffolding (finder/verifier/container/ISF) is the correct fit because criterion prompts (plan_review_*.md) are loaded as rubric text in the `instructions` field — not as system prompts — so they cannot sit above the ingested plan material; only the shared scaffold prompts are rendered as the system_prompt in every pass-1 call, making them the single right place for both the injection directive and the forward-looking rule.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define R-6 as a named, locatable rule in the criteria registry or docs (or replace the identifier with inline prose) so the affirmative-framing sweep has a verifiable decision criterion.
findings.finding: R-6 is named in the plan as an established organizing-principle identifier ('R-6 (organizing principle): lead with affirmative specification…') but zero matches for 'R-6' exist anywhere in the codebase, docs, or criteria registry. It cannot be looked up, cross-referenced, or verified as a defined rule — an executor treating it as a named standard will find nothing.
findings.suggested_fix: Either add an R-6 entry to `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` or the docs with its full definition, or drop the opaque identifier and inline the three-part rule directly in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define G-12 in the criteria registry or docs (or replace the identifier with inline prose specifying the exact injection-directive text and its placement), so AC1 is verifiable.
findings.finding: G-12 is named in the plan as an established directive identifier ('G-12: one prompt-injection directive in SHARED scaffolding (system layer, above ingested material)') but zero matches for 'G-12' exist anywhere in the codebase, docs, or criteria files. It is invoked as a resolvable rule that 'applies to finder/verifier(both)/container/ISF/all agentic criteria by construction' — an undefined-but-referenced protocol the executor needs to apply.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify or create the 'shared scaffolding' artifact (name the file path and insertion mechanism) that will carry the G-12 injection directive, so AC1 has a concrete, verifiable target.
findings.finding: The plan assigns G-12's injection directive to 'SHARED scaffolding (system layer, above ingested material)' — a structural layer that does not exist in the prompt library. The only prompt with an injection-guarding section is completion_verifier.md; the plan_review_finder.md (the actual shared finder scaffold) contains no such layer. The plan neither names the file(s) that constitute this shared scaffolding nor describes how to create it.
findings.location: What section, bullet 2: 'SHARED scaffolding (system layer, above ingested material)'; AC1: 'Shared-scaffolding injection directive'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an on-fail decision rule to AC3 (e.g., 'if regression detected: revert that prompt's wording and file a targeted follow-on') so the regression check has a specified remediation path.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 ('Eval/sidecar regression check: reworded prompts still catch what they caught') names no fallback or decision rule for what happens if the regression check reveals a behavioral regression — whether to revert, re-reword, or accept the loss. An incomplete/failed sub-step has no specified remediation path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, bullet 3: 'Eval/sidecar regression check: reworded prompts still catch what they caught'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
